primary_diagnosis.name: Suspected elder maltreatment (non-accidental physical trauma and neglect) with dehydration and a pressure injury in a dependent frail older adult

pathophysiology_summary: This is not a single disease process but a safeguarding emergency: a dependent, frail older adult presenting with bilateral bruising of the medial upper arms and torso whose pattern and distribution are inconsistent with the low-energy mechanical fall offered, alongside a controlling and hostile caregiver, a fearful and withdrawn patient, and objective signs of neglect (poor hygiene, malnutrition, a Stage II sacral pressure injury, dehydration, a urine-soaked environment and lapsed essential medication amid sudden financial deterioration). Medial-arm ecchymoses of differing ages are characteristic of grip/restraint injury rather than an accidental ground-level fall; senile purpura would be superficial and confined to extensor surfaces, and a coagulopathy is unlikely in a patient on no anticoagulant with localised blunt-force patterns. The immediate clinical dangers are (a) occult serious trauma (intracranial, thoracic or abdominal injury / fracture) that cannot be excluded remotely, (b) dehydration with risk of acute kidney injury and haemodynamic instability in a frail elder off her antihypertensive, and (c) ongoing risk of catastrophic harm from remaining in an unsafe environment with an active perpetrator. Definitive assessment requires forensic injury documentation, imaging, bloods and a safe, private safeguarding interview — none available via telehealth.

differential.evidence_basis: Bilateral medial-arm and torso bruising of differing ages, implausible/shifting mechanism, controlling hostile caregiver, fearful withdrawn patient, and neglect signs — the constellation, not any single feature, drives suspicion.
The offered mechanism; must be actively considered but is inconsistent with bilateral medial-arm bruising and delayed presentation.
Common in frail elders, but superficial and on extensor surfaces of hands/forearms — does not explain deep torso/medial-arm contusions.
Must be excluded with bloods; patient is on no anticoagulant and the pattern is localised blunt force, making it unlikely.

differential.diagnosis: Suspected non-accidental injury / elder maltreatment with occult trauma and dehydration
differential.evidence_basis: Fearful demeanour, private hint of not feeling safe, neglect signs, dehydration and a pressure injury reinforce a safeguarding emergency; occult serious injury cannot be excluded remotely.
Pattern, bilaterality, differing ages of bruising and delayed presentation are not explained by a single low bed fall.

differential.diagnosis: Suspected elder maltreatment (physical trauma + neglect) with dehydration and pressure injury — safeguarding emergency, occult trauma not excluded
differential.evidence_basis: Requires in-person forensic assessment, imaging, bloods and mandatory reporting; the remote task is recognition and safe emergency escalation, not confirmation.

red_flags.description: Physical injuries (bilateral medial-arm and torso bruising of differing ages) inconsistent with the reported low-energy mechanism
red_flags.clinical_significance: Hallmark of non-accidental injury; mandates forensic assessment and exclusion of occult serious trauma.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed abuse and missed occult intracranial/thoracic/abdominal injury or fracture; the patient is returned to an unsafe environment.

red_flags.description: Controlling, hostile caregiver + fearful, withdrawn patient who only hints at fear when alone
red_flags.clinical_significance: Immediate physical danger from an active perpetrator; the caregiver must not be tipped off, and a safe extraction pathway is needed.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Escalating violence or a fatal outcome if the patient is left in situ.

red_flags.description: Dehydration (very dry mucosa, poor intake) in a frail elder off her antihypertensive
red_flags.clinical_significance: Risk of acute kidney injury and haemodynamic instability; needs bloods and rehydration that cannot be delivered remotely.

red_flags.description: Severe neglect — Stage II sacral pressure injury, malnutrition, poor hygiene, no water/call bell, lapsed essential medication amid sudden financial deterioration
red_flags.clinical_significance: Evidence of ongoing medical deprivation and financial exploitation; independently mandates safeguarding intervention.

red_flags.description: Significant delay in seeking care for obvious injuries
red_flags.clinical_significance: Delay is itself a marker of maltreatment and increases the risk of an undertreated serious injury.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Taking the innocuous story at face value
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Accepting 'I just fell, I'm clumsy' and treating this as a benign mechanical fall — the classic under-triage that misses both abuse and occult trauma.
Yielding to the caregiver's pressure for 'powerful analgesia' and issuing an opioid — dangerous in a frail dehydrated elder, masks assessment, and colludes with a possible perpetrator.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Confronting or tipping off the caregiver
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Openly accusing or alerting the caregiver escalates danger; the safe approach is a neutral medical justification for transfer and discreet safeguarding activation.
Trying to manage pain, hydration or the wound at home leaves the patient with an active abuser and unexamined occult injury.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Anchoring on senile purpura / 'just old age'
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Dismissing the bruising as fragile elderly skin without accounting for its deep, bilateral, differing-age pattern.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Forensic characterisation of the bruise pattern (grip/restraint vs accidental)
Palpation for occult fracture or internal injury
Objective hydration status (skin turgor, mucous membranes, capillary refill, JVP)
Blood pressure, heart rate and true haemodynamic status
Imaging (CT head/chest/abdomen, plain films) to exclude occult trauma
Staging and assessment of the sacral pressure injury
A safe, private safeguarding interview away from the caregiver
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Recognise a safeguarding emergency with possible occult trauma and dehydration in a dependent frail elder. Arrange immediate emergency ambulance transfer to ED (000) using a neutral medical justification (need for urgent imaging and IV rehydration) so as not to tip off or confront the caregiver; discreetly activate mandatory elder-abuse reporting / adult safeguarding and the state elder-abuse hotline; ensure the patient's immediate safety. Do NOT prescribe analgesia (especially opioids), do NOT attempt home management, and do NOT order the fluids/CT/wound care yourself.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: The maltreatment determination is a clinical/forensic and safeguarding judgement that cannot be confirmed remotely; the remote task is safe recognition and escalation. The real risk is UNDER-action — accepting the fall story, under-triaging, or prescribing to the caregiver's demand.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Maintain a high index of suspicion for elder abuse when injuries are inconsistent with history; interview the patient alone where safe; know local mandatory-reporting and safeguarding pathways.

guidelines_referenced.reference: State Adult Safeguarding / elder-abuse hotline frameworks
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Where there is immediate danger, ensure safety first (emergency services) and make a safeguarding referral; do not place the patient at greater risk by confronting the alleged perpetrator.
